verses.text: Josiah gathers all the people, reads them the book of the covenant, and stands to renew their pledge to follow the LORD with all their heart. He leads the nation back to its God. True reform is public, covenantal, and whole-hearted, not a private sentiment.

Josiah gathers all the people, reads them the book of the covenant, and stands to renew their pledge to follow the LORD with all their heart. He leads the nation back to its God. True reform is public, covenantal, and whole-hearted, not a private sentiment.

Josiah gathers all the people, reads them the book of the covenant, and stands to renew their pledge to follow the LORD with all their heart. He leads the nation back to its God. True reform is public, covenantal, and whole-hearted, not a private sentiment.

Josiah purges the temple of all the vessels of Baal and the host of heaven, burns them, and tears down the houses of the cult prostitutes. He cleanses God's house thoroughly. Real repentance does not tidy around idols but removes them root and branch.

Josiah purges the temple of all the vessels of Baal and the host of heaven, burns them, and tears down the houses of the cult prostitutes. He cleanses God's house thoroughly. Real repentance does not tidy around idols but removes them root and branch.

Josiah purges the temple of all the vessels of Baal and the host of heaven, burns them, and tears down the houses of the cult prostitutes. He cleanses God's house thoroughly. Real repentance does not tidy around idols but removes them root and branch.

Josiah purges the temple of all the vessels of Baal and the host of heaven, burns them, and tears down the houses of the cult prostitutes. He cleanses God's house thoroughly. Real repentance does not tidy around idols but removes them root and branch.

He defiles the high places from Geba to Beersheba and brings the country priests to Jerusalem. The scattered, corrupt worship is shut down. To honour God rightly, a people must end the half-pagan shrines they had grown comfortable with.

He defiles the high places from Geba to Beersheba and brings the country priests to Jerusalem. The scattered, corrupt worship is shut down. To honour God rightly, a people must end the half-pagan shrines they had grown comfortable with.

He defiles Topheth, where children were burned, removes the horses dedicated to the sun, and pulls down the rooftop altars. Even the most ingrained abominations are torn away. No sin is too entrenched for thorough repentance to uproot.

He defiles Topheth, where children were burned, removes the horses dedicated to the sun, and pulls down the rooftop altars. Even the most ingrained abominations are torn away. No sin is too entrenched for thorough repentance to uproot.

He defiles Topheth, where children were burned, removes the horses dedicated to the sun, and pulls down the rooftop altars. Even the most ingrained abominations are torn away. No sin is too entrenched for thorough repentance to uproot.

Josiah defiles even the high places Solomon himself had built for foreign gods, breaking the images and filling the sites with bones. Not even a revered king's legacy is spared. Reverence for the past must never shield what dishonours God.

Josiah defiles even the high places Solomon himself had built for foreign gods, breaking the images and filling the sites with bones. Not even a revered king's legacy is spared. Reverence for the past must never shield what dishonours God.

He destroys Jeroboam's altar at Bethel, burning bones on it as a man of God foretold three centuries before, yet spares that prophet's grave. The ancient word is fulfilled to the letter. God's prophecies wait long, but they never fail.

He destroys Jeroboam's altar at Bethel, burning bones on it as a man of God foretold three centuries before, yet spares that prophet's grave. The ancient word is fulfilled to the letter. God's prophecies wait long, but they never fail.

He destroys Jeroboam's altar at Bethel, burning bones on it as a man of God foretold three centuries before, yet spares that prophet's grave. The ancient word is fulfilled to the letter. God's prophecies wait long, but they never fail.

He destroys Jeroboam's altar at Bethel, burning bones on it as a man of God foretold three centuries before, yet spares that prophet's grave. The ancient word is fulfilled to the letter. God's prophecies wait long, but they never fail.

He removes the high places of Samaria as well, sacrificing their idolatrous priests on their own altars. The reform reaches beyond Judah's borders. Zeal for God will not leave standing the corruption it has power to remove.

He removes the high places of Samaria as well, sacrificing their idolatrous priests on their own altars. The reform reaches beyond Judah's borders. Zeal for God will not leave standing the corruption it has power to remove.

Josiah commands a Passover such as had not been kept since the days of the judges. The covenant feast is restored in its fullness. Returning to God means returning to His appointed worship, joyfully and as He prescribed.

Josiah commands a Passover such as had not been kept since the days of the judges. The covenant feast is restored in its fullness. Returning to God means returning to His appointed worship, joyfully and as He prescribed.

Josiah commands a Passover such as had not been kept since the days of the judges. The covenant feast is restored in its fullness. Returning to God means returning to His appointed worship, joyfully and as He prescribed.

He puts away the mediums and idols, so that no king before or after turned to the LORD like him, with all his heart and soul and might. His devotion is unmatched. Here is repentance as God desires it — entire and undivided.

He puts away the mediums and idols, so that no king before or after turned to the LORD like him, with all his heart and soul and might. His devotion is unmatched. Here is repentance as God desires it — entire and undivided.

Yet the LORD does not turn from His fierce wrath against Judah for all Manasseh's provocations; He will remove even Judah and this house. One godly reign cannot undo a nation's settled corruption. Reform may delay judgment, but long apostasy still bears its fruit.

Yet the LORD does not turn from His fierce wrath against Judah for all Manasseh's provocations; He will remove even Judah and this house. One godly reign cannot undo a nation's settled corruption. Reform may delay judgment, but long apostasy still bears its fruit.

Josiah is killed in battle against Pharaoh-necho at Megiddo, mourned by all. The best of kings dies untimely. The righteous are sometimes taken early — in mercy, that they may not see the evil to come.

Josiah is killed in battle against Pharaoh-necho at Megiddo, mourned by all. The best of kings dies untimely. The righteous are sometimes taken early — in mercy, that they may not see the evil to come.

Josiah is killed in battle against Pharaoh-necho at Megiddo, mourned by all. The best of kings dies untimely. The righteous are sometimes taken early — in mercy, that they may not see the evil to come.

Jehoahaz reigns three months and does evil; Pharaoh deposes him, sets up Jehoiakim, and lays a heavy tribute, which the king wrings from the people. Judah is now a vassal, milked by Egypt. The freedom forfeited by sin is replaced by foreign bondage.

Jehoahaz reigns three months and does evil; Pharaoh deposes him, sets up Jehoiakim, and lays a heavy tribute, which the king wrings from the people. Judah is now a vassal, milked by Egypt. The freedom forfeited by sin is replaced by foreign bondage.

Jehoahaz reigns three months and does evil; Pharaoh deposes him, sets up Jehoiakim, and lays a heavy tribute, which the king wrings from the people. Judah is now a vassal, milked by Egypt. The freedom forfeited by sin is replaced by foreign bondage.

Jehoiakim reigns and does evil in God's sight, like his fathers before him. The brief light of Josiah's reign is gone. After every revival, the test is whether the next generation will hold the ground gained — and this one does not.

Jehoiakim reigns and does evil in God's sight, like his fathers before him. The brief light of Josiah's reign is gone. After every revival, the test is whether the next generation will hold the ground gained — and this one does not.
